title: driver not supported
body:
I compiled buildah on fedora 26 and brought the binary over to rhel 7.3.

I had to install ostree on rhel but after that I get the the following error message

```
./buildah containers                                                                                                                                                                           
ERRO[0000] driver not supported                                                                                                                                                                                      
driver not supported
```
I'm assuming it's because I'm using overlay storage driver. Here's the relevant docker info

```
Containers: 1
 Running: 1
 Paused: 0
 Stopped: 0
Images: 222
Server Version: 17.05.0-ce
Storage Driver: overlay
 Backing Filesystem: extfs
 Supports d_type: true
Logging Driver: journald
Cgroup Driver: cgroupfs
Plugins:
 Volume: local
 Network: bridge host macvlan null overlay
Swarm: inactive
Runtimes: runc
Default Runtime: runc
Init Binary: docker-init
containerd version: 9048e5e50717ea4497b757314bad98ea3763c145
runc version: 9c2d8d184e5da67c95d601382adf14862e4f2228
init version: 949e6fa
Security Options:
 seccomp
  Profile: default
Kernel Version: 3.10.0-514.16.1.el7.x86_64
...
```

Is there a list of supported drivers or similar `buildah info` command?

comments.body:
My theory was wrong. I tried again on a system with devicemapper and get the same error.

```
$ docker info
Containers: 0                                                                                                                                                                                                        
 Running: 0
 Paused: 0
 Stopped: 0
Images: 1
Server Version: 1.12.0
Storage Driver: devicemapper
 Pool Name: docker-253:0-3425945-pool
 Pool Blocksize: 65.54 kB
 Base Device Size: 10.74 GB
 Backing Filesystem: xfs
 Data file: /dev/loop0
 Metadata file: /dev/loop1
 Data Space Used: 232.8 MB
 Data Space Total: 107.4 GB
 Data Space Available: 65.91 GB
 Metadata Space Used: 679.9 kB
 Metadata Space Total: 2.147 GB
 Metadata Space Available: 2.147 GB
 Thin Pool Minimum Free Space: 10.74 GB
 Udev Sync Supported: true
 Deferred Removal Enabled: false
 Deferred Deletion Enabled: false
 Deferred Deleted Device Count: 0
 Data loop file: /var/lib/docker/devicemapper/devicemapper/data
 WARNING: Usage of loopback devices is strongly discouraged for production use. Use `--storage-opt dm.thinpooldev` to specify a custom block storage device.
 Metadata loop file: /var/lib/docker/devicemapper/devicemapper/metadata
 Library Version: 1.02.135-RHEL7 (2016-11-16)
Logging Driver: journald
Cgroup Driver: cgroupfs
Plugins:
 Volume: local
 Network: null host bridge overlay
Swarm: inactive
Runtimes: runc
Default Runtime: runc
Security Options: seccomp
Kernel Version: 3.10.0-514.26.2.el7.x86_64
```
Why not compile and build it on RHEL?

I'm not sure what correlation there is between the configuration dockerd is using and what buildah's using.  Can you run `buildah --debug containers` to see what it's doing?  Do you have a `/etc/containers/storage.conf` file?

@rhatdan I get errors when building on RHEL which I didn't care to debug. I thought the go binary would be more or less portable but it appears it still needs packages and configuration on the host.

@nalind 
```
sudo ./buildah --debug containers                                
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay"                                                                                             
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay"                                                                                                           
ERRO[0000] driver not supported                                                      
driver not supported 
```
I do not have a /etc/containers/storage.conf file
Tested that if it's `devicemapper` in /etc/containers/storage.conf on RHEL7.3, the problem is gone.

Not sure if this issue got resolved.
Since i have setup buildah on Centos.
`$buildah version`
`>Version:       0.3
Go Version:    go1.8.3
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    b7e9966
Built:         Wed Jul 26 05:44:10 2017
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64`

I am still getting the driver not supported

Adding devicemapper to /etc/containers/storage.conf should work.  You need a 7.4 kernel for the overlay driver to work.  There is a check in the containers/storage to make sure the kernel supports SELinux and Overlay, which is what I think you are hitting.

Thanks @rhatdan I had tried adding the /etc/containers/storage.conf with `STORAGE_DRIVER="devicemapper"` although it didn't work. So i just simply exported `STORAGE_DRIVER="devicemapper"` in the console and then finally it started working.

I was able to switch to devicemapper and add this to /etc/containers/storage.conf
```
[storage]
driver = "devicemapper"
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
```
I also switched my docker engine to use devicemapper. I no longer get the error when running `buildah images`.

I also verified I could run `buildah from centos` and `buildah images` to see the image in the local storage. Thanks for the help.

I just realized the buildah storage and docker storage are completely separate configurations so there's no reason for me to change my docker storage driver.

Is there a place in my home directory I can store the storage.conf file so I don't need to rely in it being in /etc/containers?
Nope, we don't do that now.  But we should not change storage.conf going forward.  Newer versions of buildah should come with that file from the rpm.

Needing to install an rpm defeats the purpose of having a statically compiled binary. Many of the other tools I use live in my ~/bin directory and are configured from ~/.config
Packaging `buildah` in a container defeats the use case (for me) because I want to be able to build containers without needing to prepare a system (give me a portable binary) and without root (don't require SYS_ADMIN)
Is there a reason building a tar + json with `buildah` can't meet those requirements?

The fact that I already have to install ostree on a system for buildah to work is unfortunate. I was hoping it would be the portable, non-root solution I've been waiting for.

buildah right now does mount, which requires SYS_ADMIN.  We are getting closer, and you could create a conformant OCI or DOcker image with just tar and a json. 
We potentially want to look into using rpm-ostree backend which will actually handle UID!= your uid for rpm content.

Changing to  use a local config would be the easy part.
